qt:
Fill in the following blank:

Flow charts are an example of ________.
‘Specifications’ is the most important document for ______ to be read or written.
A/an _______ identifies the major steps in a project and tells when they will be performed.
A _____________ is a document that thoroughly explains a business idea and how it will be carried out.
A schedule chart identifies the major steps in a project and tells _______ they will be performed.
Writing style is a result of ________ structure.
_____________ specifications are used as the basis for the test plans and user documentation.
The best visual aid to simplify complicated data is________.
Lack of a well defined purpose leads to the experience of getting stuck while writing known as the _______ block.
Lack of confidence causes writer’s block, and it is usually _______.
Simplifying visual aids also means removing _______ details.
For many writers the worst part of the writing experience in the very…………… .
Functional specifications will form the basis for the highly precise _______ specifications.
To create a table, you systematically arrange information in rows and _______.
________ is an excellent means of representing the succession of events in a process or procedure.
In some situations, visual aids can carry the _______ message.
_____________ specifications form the basis of the highly precise design specifications.
The index forms a part of the____________.
Which of the following is correct for this definition ‘it contains an analysis of the technical issues important to the report?
In a presentation, the visual aids should go ________ the presented information.
Lack of information often leads a writer towards the production of a/an _______ text.
Black-board comes in which category of teaching-aids?
A poorly defined _______ of your communication will inevitably make writing more difficult.
...........  is a condition, primarily associated with writing as a profession, in which an author loses the ability to produce new work.
________________ is what we call the experience of getting stuck while writing.
It is commonly believed that ……………… is the manner in which a writer chooses to write to his or her audience.
What is the definition of an objective?
op:
A clearly defined and measurable outcome to be
achieved over a specified time frame.

A clear set of goals to be attained through 
a set number of resources.

qt:
Identify the following statement about Writer's Block as True or False:

Communication with a poorly defined purpose will make writing more difficult and may block it altogether.
Which of the following components is an optional addition to the front matter of an analysis report?

Choose the best option:
It includes derivations of equations, tables of raw data, sample equations, and so forth.
A/An ______ uses rectangles and lines to represent the arrangement of people and departments in an organization. It also indicates who reports to whom and who gives direction to whom.
_______ visual aids means removing unnecessary details.
In essence a …………… is a statement of the attributes of a product, process or service a user wishes to purchase, and consequently, which the supplier is expected to supply.
Which of the following is discovered during the Editing Phase?
The result of market research is called___________.
__________prose often serves as the raw material for more elaborate forms of analytical prose.
How many basic parts of a formal report are there?
To show the parts of an object you should use a__________.
_________ is used  in paragraphs to provide instances that clarify topic statement.
________  section presents the results of the analysis.
How can you integrate your visual aids with your prose so that they work together harmoniously to create a single, unified message?
op: by stating the conclusions you want your readers to draw
by making your visual aids easy to find
by introducing your visual aids in your prose

qt: Reports present conclusions based on:
_______ are unsurpassed in their ability to depict the composition of a whole.
Visual aid is an instructional aid that presents information ……………………….

Fill in the following blank:
An organizational chart uses ----- and ----- to represent the arrangement of people and departments in an organization.
What is the use of Flow Charts?
op: Flow charts are simply used to draw picture.
Flow charts are used inside the organization for writing letters.
Flow charts are an excellent means of representing the succession of events.
Flow charts are used to represent the predictions regarding business.

qt: The programmer kicked the terminal. The following sentence is an example of:
___________  specifications are used as the basis for test plans and user documentation.
____________ refer to non-alphabetical symbols.
What is the most important aspect in using visual aids?
op: your message should be understandable for your audience

qt: Excessive use of the passive voice, excessive nominalization and unnecessary words and phrases   are some common causes of ________ writing.

The programmer felt sick.
It is an example of ______ type of sentence.
If organizational problems are not fixed by the writer, the readers experience a complete breakdown in _______.
What does the acronym SMART stand for?
op:
Specific, measurable, achievable, rewarded
and timely

Specific, measurable, actionable, resourced 
and timely

qt: According to the custom, the numbers assigned to figures are usually ______.
If you do not think you can communicate effectively, you will not be able to _______.
Which section of report provides an outline of analysis reports for readers who do not wish to read the entire report?
A schedule chart identifies the major steps………………………….
op: taken by peers in a business house last year.
in the process of personality grooming activity by an individual.
in a project and tells when they will be performed.

in a business report and tells it’s
prospective.

qt: Hardware functional specifications as a rule should contain _________ characteristics.

Choose the correct option:

The title page of a report should be ----- from top to bottom and from left to right.
You can also use visual aids to present information in support of your _______ points.
Publicity is _______ promotion through media outlets.
A/An _____ is a table that shows how money will be gained or spent.
Test specifications contain one of the following:
_________ is a description of what the desired product should be capable of doing. It leads to the next type of specification.
Readability is the likelihood that a project audience will be able to _______ and comprehend a piece of documentation.
Organizational problems are discovered during the _______  phase.
The best visual aid to depict changes among variables over time is ___________.

Identify the correct sentence structure for the following sentence from the belgiven options.

"The boss smiled".
A picture is worth thousands _____
………………… is a piece of writing in which the writer uses words to convince the reader of his/her view regarding an issue.
The _________ contains an analysis of the technical issues important to the report.
If _______  analysis is performed poorly or not at all, it can cause writer’s block.
_______ is condensation of entire reports, focusing on the main issues: what was done, what was found out, and its significance.
A body infection is compared with an invading army of attacking organisms is an example of __________.
The format of a business plan should be tailored to suit its particular context.

Identify the correct sentence structure for the following sentence from the given options.

"Ali took a rime of pages".
_______ would usually include derivations of equations, tables of raw data, sample equations.
Can visual aids carry the entire message?
Report design should be _______ enough to meet a variety of writer purposes and audience needs.
By arranging the events chronologically or spatially a writer will be able to achieve __________ in his manuscript.
____________ specifications provides a step-by-step description of the testing
Agreement between subject and verb and between pronoun and its antecedent is important for paragraph ________.

Choose the correct option:
You can show your readers exactly what they would see if they personally were to look at an object.
_______ problems are discovered during the editing phase.
While using specialized visual aids, you need to consider the reader’s _________.
Which of the following is helpful for improving a document while editing?
The _______  provides an outline of analysis reports for readers who do not wish to read the entire report.
It is important to ________ the reader/audience’s attitude while making visual aids.
Drawings accurately convey the actual appearance of things whereas _______ depict subjects more abstractly.
The table is one of the most versatile and _______  used visual aids.
Which of the following statements about flowcharts is correct?
op: They may be used to depict trends over a period of time.
They are used to depict relation between variables.
They are an excellent means of representing the succession of events in a process or procedure.

qt: The basic characteristic of good specification is………………… that makes it more likely to be read, used and understood.
_________ is used in paragraphs to develop sequences that describe how an action is carried out or how something works.
____________help you to show your readers exactly what they would see if they personally were to look at an object.
Pie charts are unsurpassed in their ability to depict the composition of a __________

Fill in the following blank:

___________ charts are often used in project proposals to show the proposer’s plan of work.
Which of the following statements about visual aids is correct?
op: Titles should always be placed above the illustration.
All visual aids should be numbered the same way.
Illustrations should appeal--before they are referred to in a report.

qt:
Mark the following statement as True or False.
The table of contents provides an outline of analysis reports for readers who do not wish to read the entire report.
In which of the following sections will you place 'derivations of equations' in an analysis report?
Budget statement is the _______ that shows how money will be gained or spent.
Narration follows a __________  pattern of development in paragraph writing.
Labeling of a figure determines the level of understanding of………….
Rules for visual aids include all of the following except:
op:
Choose an appropriate aid for your audience 
and topic
Make your aid visually neat and attractive
Make your aid small and portable.

qt: The chief advantage of …………………… is realism and clarity.

Choose the correct option:
‘Appendix’ of an analysis report usually includes…
Visual aids should be selected by carefully considering the___________.
op: thesis of the speech and the size of the room
supporting material, the reasoning and the claim
audience and the purpose of the speech

qt: integrate your visual aids with your prose harmoniously to create a single _______ message.
Which visual aid best illustrates how one specific income relates to the remaining incomes?
Accurate communication is tremendously enhanced by accurate ________.
What is Requirements Specification?
op: Attempt of marketing people to specify what the market is looking for
This will form the basis for the highly precise design specifications
This is a description of what the desired product should be capable of doing

qt: Which of the following problem/s is/are discovered during 'Editing'?
The programmer quits .The following sentence is an example of:
It is commonly observed that complexity of visual aids in a document can……………… the audience to a large extent.
Which of the following visual aids effectively represents the succession of events in a process or procedure?
_______ specifications are later used as the basis for test plans and user documentation.
The ______________ section of the business plan should be written last.
__________ is a special kind of bar graph in which the bars are replaced by drawings that represent the thing being described.
The result of market research is _______ specifications.
What is the definition of a scenario in scenario planning?
op: An imagined sequence of future events

qt:
Mark the following statement as True or False:
The visual aids should be very difficult to understand.
The writer attempts to solve a problem by evaluating options and suggesting solutions in ……………….
The visual aids in brochures are often _______.
Which of following is not a way presentational aids enhance speaker confidence?
op: They give purpose to your movement.
They divert audience attention from you.
They enhance clarity and impact of your message.

qt:
Choose the correct title for the given statement:
_________ is the problem of not being able to think of something to write about or not being able to finish writing a story, poem, etc.
The glossary is the list of:
op: Technical terms used in the report

qt: A report can present the information in:
The Gunning’s Fog Index is a test designed to measure the readability of a sample of English writing.
_______help your audience find the visual aids they are looking for and know what the visual aids contain when they find them.
________ is a quick checking for errors.
Which of the following sections of an analysis report serves to orient its readers to the main issue(s) of the report?
Visual aids can be defined as______ aids, such as posters, scale models, graphs, tables, diagrams, flow charts or videotapes that present information visually.

The programmer kicked the terminal.
It is an example of _______ type of sentence.
A __________  is missing a subject, a verb, or both, but is punctuated as if it were a complete sentence.
The basic purpose of ………… is to clarify the meaning of words when voice or "body language" cues are removed.
_______ create an appropriate means to represent your subject with geometric shapes, or perhaps sketches that suggest their appearance.
Visual aids are especially important and helpful if you have to explain a ………… process to a non technical audience.
What is the chief advantage of the pictograph?
op: It is used to symbolize concretely the quantities you are talking about in the graph.
It is used to draw the attention of your executives towards your skills.
It is used to entertain your audience.

qt: Making the beliefs, values, and behavioral norms explicit and intentional builds the __________ of an organization.
To create a _______, you draw a circle and draw lines that slice it into wedges.
The length of an analysis report depends on ………………….
op: the frequency of the given task
the duration of time and data
the type and extent of the analysis

qt: In a research report, the ----- identifies an area of research, tells what is known about that area, and identifies gaps in the knowledge.
To help your readers choose between the alternative courses of action, you must evaluate the alternatives in terms of _______criteria.
An appendix is in the main body of a report.
Bibliography is one of the supplementary parts of a report.
The result of an empirical research project is its heart but it takes up very ___________ portion of the whole report.
Which of the following parts of a report includes letters of transmittal and authorization?

Fill in the blank by selecting correct subordinate conjunction.

____________ he left, he made sure he had his keys with him.
Which of the following suggests a plan of action based on findings and conclusions?
How do readers want to use the information in general reports?
op: Through social, personal and organizational problem
Through social, economic and psychological problem
Through official, legal and interpersonal problem

qt:
Choose the correct option from the following:
In report writing the summaries are often called executive summaries because they are usually addressed to……….
Making a strong statement at the beginning of a report may be misconstrued as arrogance.
Supplementary part of a report include _________________.
Which method is a bottom-up or generative approach to research?
In the ----- of a report, present the main pieces of knowledge communicated in the literature and identify some significant gap in this knowledge.
Conclusion of the investigation should be unbiased and free of personal likes and dislikes.
When you are working on …………, you will be writing to people who will make decisions, based on the results of your reports.
Conducting a survey and application of snowball technique are the procedures that can be used to collect data.
Which types of reports are used to assess opportunities, to solve problems, and to support decisions?

Choose the best option.
If he ___________ about their financial situation, he would have helped them out.

Choose the correct transitional marker from the given options.
_______ Sarah was extremely tired, she washed the dishes.
In the introduction section of an empirical report, the writer answers the reader’s question ‘Why is the research important to us?’
Writers almost always include literature reviews in the empirical research reports they write for __________.

Choose the correct option from the following:
In report writing the conclusions grow out of interpretations of the facts and recommendations grow out of ………………….
In report writing, just as conclusions grow out of interpretations of the facts, ----- grow out of conclusions.
Just as conclusions grow out of __________of the facts, _________ grow out of conclusions.
In ____________writer introduces the established facts and theories that are relevant to the writer’s work.
If a report does not have a synopsis, the letter of transmittal may summarize the major findings.
Which of these questions does not need to be asked about visual aids?
op: Does the visual show primary rather than secondary information?

qt: Which of these prefatory parts is least likely to be used?
The title ----- is a plain sheet with only the title of the report on it.
The following are typical formats for reports except

What time did you go to the movie last night

Sometimes writers briefly present all their results in one section and then discuss them in a separate section usually named as __________.
How many elements the general superstructure of reports contains?
A formal report is written in …………………

Complete the following sentence with the appropriate punctuation mark:

I wonder how old he is
Writers almost always include ___________ in the reports they write for professional journals.
Which of the following are usually addressed to decision-makers?
Readers want to use the information of a general report to solve ------------- problem/s.
In longer messages, the first few paragraphs should establish the following except
A formal report may be divided in _______________ major divisions.
In the recommendation section of an empirical report, the writer answers the reader’s question ‘What is the significance of the results?’
A literature review should be ---------.
A key part of an empirical research project is the careful formulation of the ……………… .

Choose the correct option.

Commas are used to:
op: Separate two or more independent clauses

qt: Which of the following aspects represent the function of report?
Which of the following sections suggests a plan of action based on findings and conclusions?
_______ may include tables, pictorials, graphs, charts or some additional information related to the text of a report.
Which of the following are the individual pieces of evidence that underlie and support your conclusions and recommendations?

Which of the following offers information, analysis, and recommendations?
A/An ----- is an alphabetic listing of special terms and words with their meaning and definitions.
Visual aids are used to ________ the text of any report.
An empirical research report is used to ________________
op: answer a question or test a hypothesis
state the progress made towards the project’s work
help decision-makers to choose between two or more alternatives

qt:
Choose the best option:
A professional report stresses the ____________.
op: finding of ways to make profit
details of cost on the project

qt: An essential element of every report, you prepare will be a _________in which you interpret your facts in a way that is significant to your readers.
Prefatory parts, body of report, and supplementary parts are part of ----- report.
Which of the following is not the part of terminal section of a report?
In report writing, your ----- are the individual pieces of information that underlie and support your conclusions and recommendations.

Mark as TRUE or FAlSE.
List of items mentioned in the index follows alphabetical order.
Just as conclusions grow out of interpretations of the facts, __________ grow out of conclusions.
Which of the following supplementary parts includes the list of topics, their divisions and subdivisions with their location in the report?
________ are the verifiable pieces of information that you gathered.

Choose the correct option from the following:
Which one of the following are answered in the introduction part of a report?
I'll ______________ their children while they are away on holiday.
The first step in planning a report is to
op: limit the scope of the study.

qt: Summaries addressed to decision-makers are called as ______________ summaries.

Which of the following sections answers the below mentioned reader’s question?

What results did your research produce?
Identifying your readers and their ________while writing a report will help you in how readers want to use the information you provide.
The reader of a/ an ---------- may use this report to solve an organizational, social or a personal problem.
__________ is a way to establish the importance of your research.

Choose the statement that shows the correct meaning of the following idiom
“To lead someone up the garden”
The methodology of research is independent of the nature of your research.
Which one of the following belongs to the supplementary part of a report?
Which one of the following shows the headings of a document in an outline form?

Choose the correct option from the following:
In report writing facts are the individual pieces of evidence that underlie and support your…………….
----- includes the list of topics, their divisions and subdivisions with their location in the report.
In report writing, facts are the individual pieces of information that underlie and support the conclusion and recommendation/s.
An essential element of every report is a/an _________ in which you interpret your facts in a way that is significant to your readers.
__________ refers to the sources from where the data have been collected.
______________ is a list of the books referred to in a scholarly work and it is typically part of appendix.

While writing long reports the list of symbols is ……………..
Careful construction of objectives is not important for every empirical research project.
The two important ways of presenting results are tables and _____________

Which of the following sections answers the below mentioned reader’s question?

Why is your research important to us?
Title fly is a plane sheet with only the title of the report on it.
_________ are the individual pieces of information that underlie and support the conclusions and recommendations.
The longer a message, the less effective the direct approach is in making your point.
Only ……………… are presented in technical English.
In order to explain the importance of research to your readers, it’s harmful to state the relevance of your research to your organization’s goals.
Which of the following sentences is correctly punctuated?
op: Paris, France, is my favorite place to visit.
Paris France is my favorite place, to visit.
Paris France, is my favorite place to visit.
Paris, France is my favorite place to visit.

qt: Readers want to use the information of a general report to solve personal problems only.
________ section does not simply focus on interpreting the facts but also on answering the readers’ question. i.e. “How are those facts significant to us?”

_______are the individual pieces of evidence that underlie and support your conclusions and recommendations.

A separate summary of the overall report in longer reports is often called ----- summary.
Which of the following phrases should not be used in the close of a bad-news message?
op: "We hope you will continue to do business with us."
"If you have further questions, please write."
I trust our decision is satisfactory."

qt: Which of the following report is based on observation and experiment instead of theory?

Glossary is useful for ___________.
Review of the previously published literature on the subject does not show the importance of your research.
Formal reports can contain a variety of prefatory parts, from a cover page to a synopsis or executive summary.
Which of the following documentation styles should not be used in a formal report?
op: consistent style adapted by the author of the report.

qt: _________ is used in paragraphs to provide instances that clarify topic statement.
